title: Pani Dihing Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Pani Dihing Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Assam

overview.short: Pani-Dihing Bird sanctuary is a 33.93-square-kilometre (13.10 sq mi) bird sanctuary located in Sivasagar district, Assam.
overview.description: Pani-Dihing Bird sanctuary is a 33.93-square-kilometre (13.10 sq mi) bird sanctuary located in Sivasagar district, Assam. It is 22 km (14 mi) away from Sivasagar town. This protected area was established as a Bird Sanctuary in August 1996 by the Government of Assam. It was identified as a potential bird sanctuary in late 1980s.

topPlaces.name: Charul Kora, Sivasagar
topPlaces.description: Charul Kora is a small scattered rural village in the Sivasagar tehsil, Sivasagar district, Assam state, India. In the census and other sources it is qualified as No.2 in order to distinguish it from the nearly homonymous village of Chaul Kora (No.1) in the same district, about 8 km to the southwest.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pani Dihing Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pani Dihing Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pani Dihing Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dibrugarh Airport (58 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (160 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pani Dihing Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pani Dihing Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pani Dihing Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Pani Dihing Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pani Dihing Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Charul Kora, Sivasagar.
